summary: IISS and Chatham House analysts assess that Strait of Hormuz tension dynamics are introducing latent risk to Mediterranean energy routing and offshore wind project financing. Expert commentary identifies a secondary concern around insider access risks at Mediterranean port facilities used for wind component logistics, driven by organised crime entanglement with port authority operations in two southern European countries.
lead_indicators: IISS: Strait of Hormuz shipping disruption scenarios re-evaluated for European energy supply chain impact
Chatham House: Organised crime infiltration of southern European port logistics poses wind component delivery risk
Foreign Affairs: Mediterranean energy infrastructure investment confidence declining among institutional investors

source_videos.title: The Mediterranean Energy Corridor: Risks in 2026
source_videos.channel: IISS — International Institute for Strategic Studies
